sections.paragraph:
τέλεον neut. acc. sing. of τέλεος (=τέλειος; cp. SibOr 3, 117) used as an adv. (Περὶ ὕψους 41, 1; Ael. Aristid. 33 p. 635 D.; Lucian, Merc. Cond. 5; Appian, Bell. Civ. 1, 8 §34; BGU 903, 12 [II a.d.]; PFay 106, 21; 3 Macc 1:22; JosAs. 12:11 cod. A [p. 56, 14 Bat.]; Jos., Bell. 4, 285) pert. to being at the end of a process, fully, altogether, in the end Dg 2:5.

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

acc. acc. = accusative

sing. sing. = singular

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 997
